page: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The configuration provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. A key characteristic of this instance is its massive memory allocation of 256.0 GB, translating to 32.0 GB of RAM per core, which is designed for memory-intensive workloads.

This server does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage, which the instance is capable of supporting. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The CPU features a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, alongside hardware-assisted virtualization and advanced vector extensions such as AVX-512.

Performance benchmarks place the E32-8s_v4 consistently in the average tier for most operations. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1247.0 and a multi-core score of 4779.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 415.81 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 45.36 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database-related tests, it handles 7.06 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 2961.05 on PassMark database operations. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak at 28.42 GB/sec, and medium-sized LLM inference using a 7B model is poor, yielding only 12.42 tokens/sec for prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32-8s_v4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a specialized choice where RAM capacity is prioritized over raw compute density. This profile is highly suitable for in-memory databases, caching layers, large-scale data processing, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory footprints without needing a high count of processing cores.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv4 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, offering 8 dedicated vCPUs and an exceptionally large memory capacity of 256.0 GB. This configuration yields 32.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show average performance across most tests, including 7.06 million Redis SET operations per second, a Geekbench multi-core score of 4779.0, and a PassMark memory score of 2635.49. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and medium LLM inference. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring high memory density relative to compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage support, ideal for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v4 is an Intel-powered virtual machine offering 8 vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring an exceptional memory-to-core ratio designed for high-capacity database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Extremely high memory capacity of 256.0 GB (32.0 GB per core)
Premium Storage capable with no local storage bundled
Delivers 7.06 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy workloads
